(P349) == * 01161737 == Commons category (P373) == * Sasaki-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県近江八幡市安土町常楽寺1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sasaki-jinja}} [[JA:沙沙貴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= 沙沙貴神社 |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地= {{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|近江八幡市|Q385461}}{{jalink|安土町地域自治区|Q11450461|安土町}}常楽寺1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|少彦名命}}、{{jalink|大彦命|Q11435420}}（大毘古神）、{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}（大鷦鷯尊）、{{jalink|宇多天皇|Q349267}}・{{jalink|敦実親王|Q11499521}}。これらの神を総称して佐佐木大明神という。 |神体= |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建= 伝・神代 |本殿= 五間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}銅板葺 |別名= |札所等= |例祭= 近江源氏祭（10月） |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=沙沙貴神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''沙沙貴神社'''（ささきじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|近江八幡市|Q385461}}{{jalink|安土町地域自治区|Q11450461|安土町}}常楽寺にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 === 概要 === [[画像:Sasaki jinja01s2400.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|楼門}}]] {{jalink|少彦名命}}を含めて計四座五柱の神々を祀り、「'''佐佐木大明神'''」と総称する。{{jalink|近江源氏|Q11638504}}の氏神であり、{{jalink|佐々木|Q20011803}}姓発祥地に鎮座する。 === 歴史 === 神代に{{jalink|少彦名神}}が{{jalink|小豆}}に似た豆のサヤである「ササゲ」の船に乗って海を渡り、当地に降り立ったという。このことからこの地は「ササキ」と呼ばれるようになり、その地に少彦名神を祀ったことが当社の始まりであるという。古代に{{jalink|沙沙貴山君|Q11553073}}が{{jalink|大彦命|Q11435420}}を合わせて祀り、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}が{{jalink|高穴穂宮|Q109367262|志賀高穴穂宮}}への遷都に際して大規模な社殿を造営させたと伝わる。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}には{{jalink|近江国|Q843882}}{{jalink|蒲生郡|Q931658}}の{{jalink|式内社}}となっている。 {{jalink|宇多源氏|Q4846201}}の{{jalink|源成頼|Q11564102}}がこの地に土着するようになると、孫の{{jalink|佐々木経方|Q11382364}}が{{jalink|佐々木氏|Q1028587}}と称するようになった。そして、もともとこの地に住んでいた沙沙貴山君の子孫である沙沙貴氏と次第に融合していくと、沙沙貴氏の{{jalink|氏神|Q767655}}であった沙沙貴神社に宇多源氏の祖である{{jalink|敦実親王|Q11499521}}とその父・{{jalink|宇多天皇|Q349267}}を新たに合祀し、自らの氏神とした。以降当社は宇多源氏とも{{jalink|近江源氏|Q11638504}}とも呼ばれる佐々木氏の氏神とされ、子々孫々が篤く崇敬するようになった。 {{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}、この地を治めていた佐々木氏の嫡流・{{jalink|六角氏|Q2975342}}は{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}によって放逐され、当地は信長の支配下に入った。後には信長の居城である{{jalink|安土城|Q786888}}が付近に築城されたが、当社は破却を免れて維持されている。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}の{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}年間（{{jalink|1831年|Q7579}} - {{jalink|1845年|Q7636}}）に火災によって社殿が焼失するが、{{jalink|弘化|Q1325317}}5年（{{jalink|1848年|Q7647}}）に{{jalink|丸亀藩|Q10863480}}主{{jalink|京極高朗|Q11374472}}により本殿、権殿、拝殿、楼門などが再建された。その際、水原源四郎俊勇が棟梁となって平安時代、{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}の様式で再建が行われている。それらの建物はいづれも滋賀県指定{{jalink|有形文化財|Q2901860}}となっている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}時代になると、当社は{{jalink|県社}}に列せられている。 {{jalink|婆沙羅大名}}の{{jalink|佐々木道誉|Q1036907}}、信長に徹底して反抗した{{jalink|六角義賢|Q282529}}、当社を再建した{{jalink|京極高朗|Q11374472}}の他、{{jalink|日露戦争|Q159950}}での{{jalink|203高地|Q699755}}の戦いで有名な{{jalink|乃木希典|Q468122}}も佐々木一族である。現在も宇多源氏佐々木氏は、{{jalink|六角氏|Q2975342}}、{{jalink|京極氏|Q2975299}}、{{jalink|朽木氏|Q11521629}}、{{jalink|黒田氏|Q2975305}}、{{jalink|三井家|Q950465}}など220余姓の末裔の篤い信仰を集めている。 2018年9月の台風21号により本殿などが破損したが2020年5月に修復が終わった[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168　ももクロファンらが4千万円寄付　「佐々木姓氏神の神社」台風で大破も支援受け復興]京都新聞、2020年5月7日。{{jalink|ももいろクローバーZ|Q1070606}}に佐々木姓のメンバーがいることからかねてよりファンの間で聖地とされており、ファンにより修復費のうち4000万円が寄付された。 2020年6月に伐採作業中の男性が倒れた木の下敷きになって死亡する事故があった[https://www.webaminchu.jp/news/3456/　6月15日 びわ湖放送ニュース]Discover志賀　webアミンチュ、2020.6.15。 === 祭神 === * 佐佐木大明神 - 以下の神を総称していう。 ** {{jalink|少彦名命}} - 祖神・産土神。 ** {{jalink|大彦命|Q11435420}}（大毘古神） - {{jalink|沙沙貴山君|Q11553073}}の祖神で{{jalink|四道将軍|Q9076804}}の一人。 ** {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}}（大鷦鷯尊） - 沙沙貴にゆかりある祭神。 ** {{jalink|宇多天皇|Q349267}}・{{jalink|敦実親王|Q11499521}} - {{jalink|宇多源氏|Q4846201}}佐々木氏の祖神。 === 境内 === [[File:Nogisan okotoba.jpg|thumb|乃木将軍の言葉]] [[File:Sasakihjinzya keidai.jpg|thumb|なんじゃもんじゃの木]] * 本殿（滋賀県指定有形文化財） - {{jalink|弘化|Q1325317}}5年（{{jalink|1848年|Q7647}}）に{{jalink|丸亀藩|Q10863480}}主{{jalink|京極高朗|Q11374472}}により再建。建築物の建築構造は五間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}、向拝三間、銅板葺。 * 透塀（滋賀県指定有形文化財） * 幣殿 * 拝殿（滋賀県指定有形文化財） - 弘化5年（1848年）に京極高朗により再建。桁行・梁間とも三間、方形。 * 磐境 - 祭神：{{jalink|少彦名命}}、{{jalink|沙沙貴山君|Q11553073}} * 中門（滋賀県指定有形文化財） * 権殿（滋賀県指定有形文化財） - 中央の座祭神：置目姫命（おきめひめのみこと）・狭狭城山君の祖倭帒（やまとふくろ）宿禰の妹、{{jalink|源雅信|Q3314711}}公、源秀義（{{jalink|佐々木秀義|Q7424985}}）公、源氏頼（{{jalink|六角氏頼|Q11392545}}）公。東の座祭神：{{jalink|乃木希典|Q468122}}命、乃木静子命。西の座祭神：沙沙貴郷、旧{{jalink|蒲生郡|Q931658}}の{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}・{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}・{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}での戦没者参千七百余柱命。弘化5年（1848年）に京極高朗により再建。 * 乃木大将お手植えの松。 * 宝蔵 * 神輿蔵 * 祭器庫 * 海童神社 * 影友{{jalink|稲荷神社|Q514480}} * {{jalink|加茂神社|Q11398885}} * {{jalink|愛宕神社|Q713021}} * {{jalink|祇園社}} - 祭神：八坂大神、津島大神 * 歳徳神社（恵方之社） - 祭神：{{jalink|太歳神|Q10941586|歳徳神}}（としとくじん）。毎年、社の正面をその年の恵方の方角に向けて変えている。 * {{jalink|十二支|Q1072961}}の石造群 * {{jalink|干支|Q246658}}の庭 - 中嶋登茂美作。 * {{jalink|さざれ石|Q11267921}}の庭 * 石灯籠（彌陀六燈籠、近江八幡市指定有形文化財） * 絵馬殿 * 庭園「呑月（どんげつ）の庭」 - {{jalink|1889年|Q7831}}（明治22年）、勝元宗益による築庭。 * 東廻廊（滋賀県指定有形文化財） - 弘化5年（1848年）に京極高朗により再建。 * 西廻廊（滋賀県指定有形文化財） - 弘化5年（1848年）に京極高朗により再建。 * 楼門（滋賀県指定有形文化財） - {{jalink|延享|Q1183728}}4年（{{jalink|1747年|Q7596}}）再建。平安時代の様式。葭葺、二層門。扁額「沙沙貴神社」は{{jalink|有栖川宮熾仁親王|Q668338}}の筆。 * 社務所 * 物品庫 * 旧源照殿（国{{jalink|登録有形文化財|Q11579194}}） - 元々は、{{jalink|1919年|Q2157}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年）に東近江市{{jalink|五個荘町|Q5578053|五個荘}}金堂（こんどう）地区の外村（とのむら）家に建てられた、皇族に縁がある貴人用の宿泊所である。後に現在地に移築された。 * {{jalink|ナンジャモンジャ|Q11324098|なんじゃもんじゃ}}の木 * 百華苑 * 境外社 ** {{jalink|若宮神社|Q11616280}} ** {{jalink|天満宮|Q798838}} ** {{jalink|八幡神社}} ** {{jalink|恵比須神社}} ** {{jalink|琴平神社}} ** {{jalink|津島神社|Q705136}} === 文化財 === === 国登録有形文化財 === * 沙沙貴神社旧源照殿 === 滋賀県指定有形文化財 === * 本殿 * 中門 * 透塀 * 権殿 * 拝殿 * 楼門 * 東回廊 * 西回廊 === 近江八幡市指定有形文化財 === * 石灯籠 === 祭事 === * 近江源氏祭（10月） === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR}}{{jalink|東海道本線|Q1190152}}（{{jalink|琵琶湖線|Q804135}}）{{jalink|安土駅|Q649689}}より徒歩10分 * 同駅または{{jalink|近江八幡駅|Q1042782}}（JR東海道本線(琵琶湖線)・{{jalink|近江鉄道八日市線|Q7081107}}(万葉あかね線)）から{{jalink|あかこんバス|Q17190918}}で沙沙貴神社停留所下車 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|佐々木氏|Q1028587}} * {{jalink|宇多源氏|Q4846201}} * {{jalink|近江源氏|Q11638504}} * {{jalink|出雲源氏|Q11395897}} * {{jalink|天河伝説殺人事件 (映画)|Q11442750}} ‐ 撮影地として使用された === 外部リンク === {{Commons|Category:Sasaki-jinja}} * [http://sasakijinja.or.jp/ 沙沙貴神社・佐佐木大明神] * [http://www.azuchi-shiga.com/mizu-sasakijinjya.htm 安土町観光協会・沙沙貴神社] {{ja icon}} {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ささきしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=滋賀県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sasaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sasaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:佐々木氏|社ささきしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:近江八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:滋賀県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Ōmihachiman|Q385461}}{{jalink|Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} and {{jalink|Prince Atsumi|Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former rank was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{jalink|Ōmi Genji|Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{jalink|Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102}} of the {{jalink|Uda Genji|Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{jalink|Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}. Later {{jalink|Azuchi Castle|Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{jalink|1848|Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{jalink|Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} ** {{jalink|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420}} ** {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} ** {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} / {{jalink|Prince Atsumi|Q11499521}} === Precincts === * Main sanctuary (Shiga designated tangible cultural property) * Sukibei (fence) * Heiden * Worship hall * Iwasaka * Chūmon * Gonden * Rōmon * East/West corridors * Former Genshōden (National Registered Tangible Cultural Property) * Various subsidiary shrines and monuments === Cultural properties === === National Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Former Genshōden of Sasaki Shrine === Shiga Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main sanctuary * Chūmon * Sukibei * Gonden * Worship hall * Rōmon * East corridor * West corridor === Ōmihachiman City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * Stone lantern === Festival === * Ōmi Genji Festival (October) === Access === * 10 min walk from {{jalink|Azuchi Station|Q649689}} on JR Biwako Line * Akakon bus stop “Sasaki Shrine” === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Sasaki clan|Q1028587}} * {{jalink|Uda Genji|Q4846201}} * {{jalink|Ōmi Genji|Q11638504}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Sasaki-jinja}} * [http://sasakijinja.or.jp/ Sasaki Shrine / Sasaki Daimyōjin] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ささきしんしや}} Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Ōmihachiman|Q385461}}{{jalink|Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} and {{jalink|Prince Atsumi|Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former rank was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{jalink|Ōmi Genji|Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{jalink|Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102}} of the {{jalink|Uda Genji|Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{jalink|Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}. Later {{jalink|Azuchi Castle|Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{jalink|1848|Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{jalink|Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Ōmihachiman|Q385461}}{{jalink|Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} and {{jalink|Prince Atsumi|Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former rank was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{jalink|Ōmi Genji|Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{jalink|Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102}} of the {{jalink|Uda Genji|Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{jalink|Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}. Later {{jalink|Azuchi Castle|Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{jalink|1848|Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{jalink|Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} ** {{jalink|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420}} ** {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} ** {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} / {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Ōmihachiman|Q385461}}{{jalink|Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} and {{jalink|Prince Atsumi|Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former rank was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{jalink|Ōmi Genji|Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{jalink|Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102}} of the {{jalink|Uda Genji|Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{jalink|Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}. Later {{jalink|Azuchi Castle|Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{jalink|1848|Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{jalink|Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Ōmihachiman|Q385461}}{{jalink|Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} and {{jalink|Prince Atsumi|Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former rank was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{jalink|Ōmi Genji|Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{jalink|Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102}} of the {{jalink|Uda Genji|Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{jalink|Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}. Later {{jalink|Azuchi Castle|Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{jalink|1848|Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{jalink|Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{jalink|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} ** {{jalink|Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420}} ** {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} ** {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} / {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill||lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor)
former {{ill||lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}; former rank was {{ill||lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill||lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill||lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} / {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521}} === Precincts === * Main sanctuary (Shiga designated tangible cultural property) * Sukibei (fence) * Heiden * Worship hall * Iwasaka * Chūmon * Gonden * Rōmon * East/West corridors * Former Genshōden (National Registered Tangible Cultural Property) * Various subsidiary shrines and monuments === Cultural properties === === National Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Former Genshōden of Sasaki Shrine === Shiga Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main sanctuary * Chūmon * Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} / {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521}} === Precincts === * Main sanctuary (Shiga designated tangible cultural property) * Sukibei (fence) * Heiden {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} ** {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275687}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} / {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521}} === Precincts === * Main sanctuary (Shiga designated tangible cultural property) * Sukibei (fence) * Heiden * Worship hall * Iwasaka * Chūmon * Gonden * Rōmon * East/West corridors * Former Genshōden (National Registered Tangible Cultural Property) * Various subsidiary shrines and monuments === Cultural properties === === National Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Former Genshōden of Sasaki Shrine === Shiga Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main sanctuary * Chūmon * Sukibei * Gonden * Worship hall * Rōmon * East corridor * West corridor === Ōmihachiman City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * Stone lantern === Festival === * Ōmi Genji Festival (October) === Access === * 10 min walk from {{ill|lt=Azuchi Station|Q649689|WD=Q649689}} on JR Biwako Line * Akakon bus stop “Sasaki Shrine” === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sasaki clan|Q1028587|WD=Q1028587}} * {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504}} === External links === [[JA:沙沙貴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi|1=Azuchi-cho Regional Autonomous District}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102|1=Sasaki Nariyori}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364|1=Sasaki Tsunekata}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888|1=Azuchi Castle}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647|1=1848}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472|1=Kyōgoku Takaakira}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * [[JA:沙沙貴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi|1=Azuchi-cho Regional Autonomous District}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102|1=Sasaki Nariyori}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364|1=Sasaki Tsunekata}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888|1=Azuchi Castle}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647|1=1848}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472|1=Kyōgoku Takaakira}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} / {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}} === Precincts === * Main sanctuary (Shiga designated tangible cultural property) * Sukibei (fence) * Heiden * Worship hall * Iwasaka * Chūmon * Gonden * Rōmon * East/West corridors * Former Genshōden (National Registered Tangible Cultural Property) * Various subsidiary shrines and monuments === Cultural properties === === National Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Former Genshōden of Sasaki Shrine === Shiga Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main sanctuary * Chūmon * Sukibei * Gonden * Worship hall * Rōmon * East corridor * West corridor === Ōmihachiman City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * Stone lantern === Festival === * Ōmi Genji Festival (October) === Access === * 10 min walk from {{ill|lt=Azuchi Station|Q649689|WD=Q649689|1=Azuchi Station}} on JR Biwako Line Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi|1=Azuchi-cho Regional Autonomous District}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102|1=Sasaki Nariyori}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364|1=Sasaki Tsunekata}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888|1=Azuchi Castle}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647|1=1848}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472|1=Kyōgoku Takaakira}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} / {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}} === Precincts === * Main sanctuary (Shiga designated tangible cultural property) * Sukibei (fence) * Heiden * Worship hall * Iwasaka * Chūmon * Gonden * Rōmon * East/West corridors * Former Genshōden (National Registered Tangible Cultural Property) * Various subsidiary shrines and monuments === Cultural properties === === National Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Former Genshōden of Sasaki Shrine === Shiga Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main sanctuary * Chūmon * Sukibei * Gonden * Worship hall * Rōmon * East corridor * West corridor === Ōmihachiman City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * Stone lantern === Festival === * Ōmi Genji Festival (October) === Access === * 10 min walk from {{ill|lt=Azuchi Station|Q649689|WD=Q649689|1=Azuchi Station}} on JR Biwako Line * Akakon bus stop “Sasaki Shrine” === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sasaki clan|Q1028587|WD=Q1028587|1=Sasaki clan}} * {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Sasaki-jinja}} * [http://sasakijinja.or.jp/ Sasaki Shrine / Sasaki Daimyōjin] {{神道 横}} [[JA:沙沙貴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi|1=Azuchi-cho Regional Autonomous District}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102|1=Sasaki Nariyori}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364|1=Sasaki Tsunekata}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888|1=Azuchi Castle}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647|1=1848}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472|1=Kyōgoku Takaakira}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} / {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}} === Precincts === * Main sanctuary (Shiga designated tangible cultural property) * Sukibei (fence) * Heiden * Worship hall * Iwasaka * Chūmon * Gonden * Rōmon * East/West corridors * Former Genshōden (National Registered Tangible Cultural Property) * Various subsidiary shrines and monuments === Cultural properties === === National Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Former Genshōden of Sasaki Shrine === Shiga Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main sanctuary * Chūmon * Sukibei * Gonden * Worship hall * Rōmon * East corridor * West corridor === Ōmihachiman City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * Stone lantern === Festival === * Ōmi Genji Festival (October) === Access === * 10 min walk from {{ill|lt=Azuchi Station|Q649689|WD=Q649689|1=Azuchi Station}} on JR Biwako Line * Akakon bus stop “Sasaki Shrine” === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sasaki clan|Q1028587|WD=Q1028587|1=Sasaki clan}} * {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Sasaki-jinja}} * [http://sasakijinja.or.jp/ Sasaki Shrine / Sasaki Daimyōjin] {{神道 横}} [[JA:沙沙貴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi|1=Azuchi-cho Regional Autonomous District}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102|1=Sasaki Nariyori}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364|1=Sasaki Tsunekata}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888|1=Azuchi Castle}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647|1=1848}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472|1=Kyōgoku Takaakira}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} / {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}} === Precincts === * Main sanctuary (Shiga designated tangible cultural property) * Sukibei (fence) * Heiden * Worship hall * Iwasaka * Chūmon * Gonden * Rōmon * East/West corridors * Former Genshōden (National Registered Tangible Cultural Property) * Various subsidiary shrines and monuments === Cultural properties === === National Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Former Genshōden of Sasaki Shrine === Shiga Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main sanctuary * Chūmon * Sukibei * Gonden * Worship hall * Rōmon * East corridor * West corridor === Ōmihachiman City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * Stone lantern === Festival === * Ōmi Genji Festival (October) === Access === * 10 min walk from {{ill|lt=Azuchi Station|Q649689|WD=Q649689|1=Azuchi Station}} on JR Biwako Line * Akakon bus stop “Sasaki Shrine” === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sasaki clan|Q1028587|WD=Q1028587|1=Sasaki clan}} * {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Sasaki-jinja}} * [http://sasakijinja.or.jp/ Sasaki Shrine / Sasaki Daimyōjin] {{神道 横}} [[JA:沙沙貴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi|1=Azuchi-cho Regional Autonomous District}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102|1=Sasaki Nariyori}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364|1=Sasaki Tsunekata}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888|1=Azuchi Castle}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647|1=1848}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472|1=Kyōgoku Takaakira}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} / {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}} === Precincts === * Main sanctuary (Shiga designated tangible cultural property) * Sukibei (fence) * Heiden * Worship hall * Iwasaka * Chūmon * Gonden * Rōmon * East/West corridors * Former Genshōden (National Registered Tangible Cultural Property) * Various subsidiary shrines and monuments === Cultural properties === === National Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Former Genshōden of Sasaki Shrine === Shiga Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main sanctuary * Chūmon * Sukibei * Gonden * Worship hall * Rōmon * East corridor * West corridor === Ōmihachiman City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * Stone lantern === Festival === * Ōmi Genji Festival (October) === Access === * 10 min walk from {{ill|lt=Azuchi Station|Q649689|WD=Q649689|1=Azuchi Station}} on JR Biwako Line * Akakon bus stop “Sasaki Shrine” === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sasaki clan|Q1028587|WD=Q1028587|1=Sasaki clan}} * {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Sasaki-jinja}} * [http://sasakijinja.or.jp/ Sasaki Shrine / Sasaki Daimyōjin] {{神道 横}} [[JA:沙沙貴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553074}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Sasaki Shrine |画像= [[File:Sasaki-jinja, shaden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}{{ill|lt=Azuchi Town district|Q11450461|WD=Q11450461|lt=Azuchi|1=Azuchi-cho Regional Autonomous District}} Jōrakuji 1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} (Ōbiko-no-Kami), {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (Ōsasagi-no-Mikoto), {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} and {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}}. Collectively called Sasaki Daimyōjin. |神体= |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= tradition: age of the gods |本殿= Five-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Ōmi Genji Festival (October) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Sasaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sasaki Shrine''' (ささきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Jōrakuji, Azuchi, Ōmihachiman, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === It enshrines five deities in four seats including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}, collectively called “Sasaki Daimyōjin.” It is the clan shrine of the {{ill|lt=Ōmi Genji|Q11638504|WD=Q11638504|1=Omi Genji}} and stands at the birthplace of the Sasaki surname. === History === Tradition says Sukunahikona crossed the sea in a bean-pod boat and landed here in the age of the gods. From this the area came to be called “Sasaki,” and enshrining Sukunahikona there became the shrine’s origin. In the Heian period it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in Gamō District, Ōmi Province. When {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Naruyori|Q11564102|WD=Q11564102|1=Sasaki Nariyori}} of the {{ill|lt=Uda Genji|Q4846201|WD=Q4846201|1=Uda Genji}} settled here, his grandson {{ill|lt=Sasaki Tsunekata|Q11382364|WD=Q11382364|1=Sasaki Tsunekata}} took the Sasaki name. As fusion advanced with local Sasaki clan descendants, they newly enshrined Prince Atsumi and Emperor Uda and made this their clan shrine. In the Sengoku period, the ruling Rokkaku line was expelled by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}. Later {{ill|lt=Azuchi Castle|Q786888|WD=Q786888|1=Azuchi Castle}} was built nearby, but the shrine survived. In the Tenpō era, fire destroyed buildings. In {{ill|lt=1848|Q7647|WD=Q7647|1=1848}} (Kōka 5), Marugame lord {{ill|lt=Kyōgoku Takaakira|Q11374472|WD=Q11374472|1=Kyōgoku Takaakira}} rebuilt the main sanctuary, gonden, worship hall, and rōmon in Heian/Kamakura style. These are now Shiga-designated tangible cultural properties. In September 2018, Typhoon 21 damaged the main sanctuary etc.; repairs were completed in May 2020[https://www.kyoto-np.co.jp/articles/-/236168 Kyoto Shimbun, May 7, 2020]. === Enshrined deities === * Sasaki Daimyōjin (collective name) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} ** {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|WD=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} / {{ill|lt=Prince Atsumi|Q11499521|WD=Q11499521|1=Atsumi-shinnō}} === Precincts === * Main
